name: HTS_CUSTOM_SHIFT_TYPES_B
description: Table to store Shift related data.

columns.Name: SHIFT_TYPE_ID
columns.Comments: Shift identifier to uniquely identify a shift

columns.Name: START_TIME_MS_NUM
columns.Comments: Start Time for shift detail in milliseconds from midnight.

columns.Name: END_TIME_MS_NUM
columns.Comments: End Time for shift in milliseconds from mignight

columns.Name: DURATION_MS_NUM
columns.Comments: Duration for shift detail in milliseconds from start time.

columns.Name: DURATION_UOM_CODE
columns.Comments: Unit of measure in which duration is entered for a Shift.

columns.Name: SHIFT_TYPE_CODE
columns.Comments: A Shift can have following types - Time, Duration and Elapsed. Time shifts are used to track fixed work timings. They have a specific start time and end time. Duration shifts are used when the resource does not have a fixed start time and end time but rather works for certain number of hours, is off for some hours, again works for some number of hours and so on. Elapsed shifts are used when a resource can work a set number of hours anytime during the day.

columns.Name: SHORT_TXT
columns.Comments: Short Code can be used to display a Shift when visualizing a Schedule. Since this is a three letter code it takes less space than the Name.

columns.Name: CATEGORY_CODE
columns.Comments: Category can be used to group Shifts.

columns.Name: GLOBAL_FLAG
columns.Comments: GLOBAL_FLAG

columns.Name: AVAILABILITY_CODE
columns.Comments: Period Type defines whether the resource is working (Work Period) or not working (Off Period) during the Shift.

columns.Name: SHIFT_DTL_TYPE_CODE
columns.Comments: Shift Slices divide the Shift into further intervals. Slice types can be Punch (having fixed start and end times) and Flex (having flexible start and end times).

columns.Name: ENTERPRISE_ID
columns.Comments: ENTERPRISE_ID

columns.Name: DELETED_FLAG
columns.Comments: This column is to support soft delete. Possible values 'Y' or 'N'.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: SHIFT_SHORT_NAME
columns.Comments: SHIFT_SHORT_NAME

columns.Name: SHIFT_COLOUR
columns.Comments: SHIFT_COLOUR

columns.Name: START_EARLY_GRACE
columns.Comments: Clock integration Start Early grace period for violation

columns.Name: START_LATE_GRACE
columns.Comments: Clock integration Start late grace period for violation

columns.Name: END_EARLY_GRACE
columns.Comments: Clock integration End early grace period for violation

columns.Name: END_LATE_GRACE
columns.Comments: Clock integration End late grace period for violation

columns.Name: TOLERANCE
columns.Comments: Clock integration Threshold to ignore violation

columns.Name: START_EARLY_VIOLATION
columns.Comments: Clock integration Start Early violation message

columns.Name: START_LATE_VIOLATION
columns.Comments: Clock integration Start late violation message

columns.Name: END_EARLY_VIOLATION
columns.Comments: Clock integration End early violation message

columns.Name: END_LATE_VIOLATION
columns.Comments: Clock integration End late violation message

columns.Name: TIME_NOT_WORKED
columns.Comments: TIME_NOT_WORKED

columns.Name: DAY_INDICATOR
columns.Comments: Day indicator for next day and previous day w.r.t ref date

columns.Name: SHIFT_CODE
columns.Comments: SHIFT_CODE

columns.Name: MODULE_ID
columns.Comments: Seed Data Framework: indicates the module that owns the row. A module is an entry in Application Taxonomy such as a Logical Business Area. When the MODULE_ID column exists and the owner of the row is not specified, then the Seed Data Framework will not extract the row as seed data.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: SGUID
columns.Comments: The seed global unique identifier. Oracle internal use only.

columns.Name: SEED_DATA_SOURCE
columns.Comments: Source of seed data record. A value of 'BULK_SEED_DATA_SCRIPT' indicates that record was bulk loaded. Otherwise, specifies the name of the seed data file.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET1
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET1. Context values are Y or N.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET2
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET2. Context values are Y or N.
